evaluation.summary: Higher-order 3-gram analysis in Base-2 yields a KL divergence of 0.00 between primes and composites, confirming another null finding with no detectable distributional difference in binary digit sequences.
next_directions: Extend n-gram analysis to higher bases (Base-10, Base-16) where digit diversity is greater and distributional differences may emerge
Investigate inter-prime gap sequences or positional n-gram patterns rather than digit-level statistics
Apply n-gram analysis to prime residues modulo small primes, where known structural biases (e.g., Chebyshev's bias) may produce detectable signals
